user_query:
We need a shared, ranked dataset to coordinate our volunteers around official public notices and meetings related to logging permits and forest management in Oregon. Using the provided inputs, please discover, download, extract, filter, and rank relevant notices from official sources, and produce the following outputs under the workspace.

Inputs (provided in this workspace):
- input/priority_areas.csv (list of Oregon forests and counties we focus on)
- input/keyword_weights.json (weights for keyword-based relevance scoring)
- input/exclude_domains.txt (domains to exclude from search results)

Requirements:
1) Use a web search engine to discover relevant pages. Build queries by combining each area_name from input/priority_areas.csv with terms such as: public notice, public comment, meeting, hearing, permit, logging, timber, harvest, timber sale, forest management plan. Only retain results from official sources, specifically: government domains (.gov, .state.or.us, oregon.gov), federal land management domains (blm.gov, usda.gov/forestservice), county/city government domains in Oregon, or internationally recognized certification bodies (fsc.org, pefc.org). Exclude domains listed in input/exclude_domains.txt and avoid news sites, social media, and general blogs.
2) Download each relevant page and save the raw HTML under data/raw_pages/ as individual .html files. Keep at least 10 pages spanning at least 3 distinct official domains. Also write a tab-separated search log at logs/search_log.tsv with columns: timestamp_utc, area_name, query, urls_saved_count, domains_saved (semicolon-separated, unique for that query).
3) Extract notices from the downloaded pages. For each page, parse items related to logging permits, timber harvests/sales, forest management plans, public notices/comments/meetings/hearings. For each extracted item, capture at least: source_url, source_domain, page_title, notice_title, notice_date (ISO 8601 if determinable), location (if present), text_excerpt (a short excerpt containing the matched terms), and keywords_matched (semicolon-separated list of matched keywords from input/keyword_weights.json). Save one JSON object per line in data/extracted/notices.jsonl. Skip items without any identifiable date.
4) Filter and deduplicate. Keep only items with notice_date within the next 12 months from today and whose text or location contains any area_name from input/priority_areas.csv (case-insensitive). Deduplicate by normalized tuple (notice_title, notice_date, source_domain). Save the filtered set as data/filtered/filtered_notices.csv with columns in this order: id, source_url, source_domain, notice_title, notice_date, location, matched_areas (semicolon-separated), relevance_score, keywords_matched. Define id deterministically as a stable hash or slug of source_url + notice_title + notice_date.
5) Rank. Compute relevance_score for each filtered item as the weighted sum of occurrences of each keyword in input/keyword_weights.json found in (notice_title + text_excerpt + location), case-insensitive, plus a bonus of +2 for each priority area name that appears in the notice_title. When sorting, order by: relevance_score (desc), then notice_date (asc), then notice_title (asc, case-insensitive). Save the top 20 items (or all if fewer) to reports/top_20.csv with the same columns as data/filtered/filtered_notices.csv.
6) Summary. Write logs/summary.json including: total_pages_downloaded, distinct_domains_count, total_notices_extracted, filtered_notices_count, top_domains (array of up to 10 domains with counts), and a boolean field coverage_ok indicating whether pages >= 10 and distinct_domains >= 3. Ensure filtered_notices_count matches the number of rows in data/filtered/filtered_notices.csv.

Notes:
- Do not hardcode any specific URLs.
- Use only the official-domain criteria above to select sources.
- All timestamps should be ISO 8601 UTC.
- The task is complete when all listed outputs exist and adhere to the field/format constraints.

Expected outputs and paths:
- logs/search_log.tsv
- data/raw_pages/*.html (one per page saved)
- data/extracted/notices.jsonl
- data/filtered/filtered_notices.csv
- reports/top_20.csv
- logs/summary.json All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
